How Do Minimum Wages Affect Workplace Injuries?", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["URL Source: https://research.upjohn.org/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1093&context=up_policybriefs Published Time: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 14:04:47 GMT Number of Pages: 5 Markdown Content: Upjohn Research Upjohn Institute Policy and Research Briefs Upjohn Res", "Jisung Park, and Anna Stansbury. 2026. \"How Do Minimum Wages Affect Workplace Injuries?\" Policy and Research Brief.", "Upjohn Institute for Employment Research. https://doi.org/10.17848/pb2026-94 This title is brought to you by the Upjohn Institute.", "These could include workplace amenities, scheduling, or health and safety.", "In the United States, about 2.5 million people are hurt on the job each year, and over 5,000 are killed."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: URL Source: https://research.upjohn.org/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1093&context=up_policybriefs Published Time: Thu, 19"}, {"url": "https://mitsloan.mit.edu/centers-initiatives/hsi/minimum-wage-increases-and-workplace-injuries-evidence-firm-responses-to-higher-labor-costs", "title": "Minimum Wage Increases and Workplace Injuries", "summary": "MIT Sloan Health Systems Initiative Research Minimum Wage Increases and Workplace Injuries: Evidence on Firm Responses to Higher Labor Costs Debates over minimum wage policy have historically centered on a relatively narrow set of outcomes, most notably employment effects and wage gains. This focus, while analytically expedient, risks obscuring a broader set of firm responses that operate through non-wage aspects of work. A recent study co-authored by Professor Anna Stansbury contributes to a growing literature that examines these non-wage margins.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Using administrative data on more than 13 million workers\u2019 compensation claims in California between 2000 and 2019, the authors document a consistent, quantitatively meaningful relationship: increases in the minimum wage are associated with higher wo", "A 10% increase in the minimum wage leads to approximately two to three additional injuries per 1,000 low-wage workers annually.", "Faced with higher wage bills, firms may economize on maintenance, training, or protective equipment.", "The authors estimate that the welfare cost of increased workplace injuries reduces the net benefit of higher wages by approximately 10% to 20%.", "If minimum wage increases generate incentives for work intensification, complementary policies, such as enhanced workplace safety enforcement or incentives for capital investment in safer production processes, may be necessary to preserve worker welf"], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Using administrative data on more than 13 million workers\u2019 compensation claims in California between 2000 and 2019, the "}, {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11803299/", "title": "The unintended effects of a large minimum wage increase on ...", "summary": "Abstract The 2018 minimum wage increase in South Korea was a major policy change that impacted employment and labour productivity, but its effects on health have not yet been explored. The minimum wage was increased by 16.4% in January 2018, marking the largest increase over two decades and a substantial increase by international standards. While this policy change was a promise of the then-new government, the magnitude of its increase was unexpected.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Abstract The 2018 minimum wage increase in South Korea was a major policy change that impacted employment and labour productivity, but its effects on health have not yet been explored.", "The minimum wage was increased by 16.4% in January 2018, marking the largest increase over two decades and a substantial increase by international standards.", "Using a difference-in-differences design with data from the 2016 and 2018 Korean Longitudinal Study on Aging, this study focuses on individuals targeted by the minimum wage policy, particularly older adults earning the minimum wage.", "Importantly, for the period 2014 and 2016, when the minimum wage increase was relatively modest, we found positive effects on cognitive health and no negative effects on self-reported health, suggesting that negative effects on cognition emerged only", "Some argue that minimum wages lead to a decline in working hours and employment for low-waged workers (Neumark et al., 2004), while others claim that it raises the employment probability of low-wage earners as the increased minimum wage is passed on "], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Abstract The 2018 minimum wage increase in South Korea was a major policy change that impacted employment and labour pro"}, {"url": "https://ideas.repec.org/p/hcx/wpaper/2301.html", "title": "A higher minimum wage promotes workplace safety", "summary": "William Lester & Michael Reich, 2016. \"Minimum Wage Shocks, Employment Flows, and Labor Market Frictions,\" Journal of Labor Economics, University of Chicago Press, vol. 34(3), pages 663-704. - Dube, Arindrajit & Lester, T. Our Work We conducted a systematic literature review of peer-reviewed articles published in the U.S. between 2004 and 2019 that assess the effect of raising the minimum wage on employment. Our search returned 144 studies and, of those, we identified 50 that directly addressed our research question above.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Our Work We conducted a systematic literature review of peer-reviewed articles published in the U.S. between 2004 and 2019 that assess the effect of raising the minimum wage on employment.", "Eight (8) studies showed mixed or inconclusive results.", "In recent empirical studies, minimum wage raises do not appear to substantially lower employment, with many studies reporting employment effects indistinguishable from zero or random noise. 2.", "In some particular cases, raising the minimum wage may show a negative impact on employment, particularly when that wage is binding in that market or when studies restrict to examine just teen employment. 6.", "Suggested Citation: What We Know Project, Cornell University, \u201cWhat Does the Scholarly Research Say about Whether Raising the Minimum Wage Negatively Impacts Employment?\u201d (online literature review), 2020."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Our Work We conducted a systematic literature review of peer-reviewed articles published in the U.S. between 2004 and 20"}]}
